source: Distilling the Knowledge in a Neural Network.pdf

concepts.finding: Knowledge distillation transfers knowledge from a large model (or ensemble) to a small model by training on soft targets (class probabilities) using a temperature parameter to soften distributions.
concepts.implication: Distillation enables deploying lightweight student models approaching teacher accuracy, directly applicable to compressing large ranking or query understanding models for low-latency serving.
concepts.open_question: What is the optimal temperature and student architecture for distilling cross-encoder ranking models into bi-encoder retrieval models?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: A distilled bi-encoder ranking model will achieve within 3% NDCG@10 of a cross-encoder teacher while providing 100x faster inference on CPU.

concepts.finding: Specialist models (experts) trained on fine-grained confusable classes combined with a generalist model form an effective ensemble, efficiently trainable in parallel.
concepts.implication: Specialist-generalist architecture is directly applicable to search: a general ranking model + specialist models for specific query types can improve overall ranking quality.
concepts.open_question: How should specialist models be partitioned for optimal ranking quality across diverse query intents?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: A mixture of experts (1 generalist + N domain specialists) for search ranking will outperform a single generalist model by 5%+ NDCG on domain-specific queries.
